questions.prompt:
A student investigates how different light schedules affect the flowering of a short-day plant. Four groups of identical plants are kept under the same temperature, water, and soil conditions. Only the number of hours of darkness changes each day. After three weeks, the student counts the plants that flower.
Hours of darkness per day | Plants that flower out of 20
6 | 1
8 | 3
10 | 9
12 | 17
Which conclusion is best supported by the data?

choices.A: More hours of darkness were associated with more flowering.
choices.B: Plants flowered most with 6 hours of darkness.
choices.C: Darkness had no relationship to flowering.
choices.D: All plants flowered under every light schedule.

questions.correctAnswerText: More hours of darkness were associated with more flowering.
questions.explanation: The number of flowering plants increased from 1 to 17 as hours of darkness increased.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
Kinetic energy can be calculated as 1/2 × mass × speed².
Object | Mass (kg) | Speed (m/s)
A | 2 | 4
B | 4 | 3
C | 6 | 2
D | 8 | 1
Which object has the greatest kinetic energy?

choices.A: Object A
choices.B: Object B
choices.C: Object C
choices.D: Object D

questions.correctAnswerText: Object B
questions.explanation: Kinetic energy = 1/2 × mass × speed². Object B has 1/2 × 4 × 3² = 18 J, the greatest value listed.

questions.prompt:
A coastal community uses seawalls, sand dunes, and restored marshes to reduce damage from storm surge. Marshes can slow moving water, trap sediment, and provide habitat. Dunes can absorb wave energy before water reaches buildings.
Which statement best describes how these features help the community?

choices.A: They can reduce storm-surge impact by slowing or absorbing moving water.
choices.B: They stop the Moon from affecting tides.
choices.C: They prevent all future storms from forming.
choices.D: They remove all salt from ocean water.

questions.correctAnswerText: They can reduce storm-surge impact by slowing or absorbing moving water.
questions.explanation: Marshes slow water and dunes absorb wave energy, reducing storm-surge effects.

questions.prompt:
Bone tissue is living tissue. Bone cells help repair small cracks, and minerals stored in bone help make the skeleton strong. Bone marrow inside some bones produces blood cells.
Which statement best describes one function of the skeletal system?

choices.A: It supports the body and helps produce blood cells.
choices.B: It pumps air into the lungs.
choices.C: It filters urine from the blood.
choices.D: It changes sunlight into sugar.

questions.correctAnswerText: It supports the body and helps produce blood cells.
questions.explanation: Bones support the body, and marrow inside some bones produces blood cells.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A force of 18 N acts on an object and causes an acceleration of 3 m/s².
Force = mass × acceleration
What is the mass of the object in kilograms?
Type your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: Mass = force ÷ acceleration = 18 N ÷ 3 m/s² = 6 kg.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A student tests how the thickness of a rubber band affects the distance a paper airplane launcher sends a paper airplane. The same airplane is used in every trial, and the rubber band is pulled back the same distance each time.
Which TWO factors should be kept constant?

choices.A: Same paper airplane
choices.B: Pullback distance
choices.C: Rubber band thickness
choices.D: Distance traveled

questions.correctAnswerText: Same paper airplane | Pullback distance
questions.explanation: These factors should stay constant so rubber band thickness is the main variable being tested.

questions.prompt: During protein synthesis, DNA instructions are copied into messenger RNA. The messenger RNA then carries the instructions to ribosomes, where amino acids are joined to form [D1]. This process helps explain how genes can affect [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=proteins; D2=traits
questions.explanation: Genes provide instructions for making proteins, and proteins influence many traits.

questions.prompt:
A student inflates a bicycle tire. The air particles inside the tire collide with the tire walls. As more air is pumped into the same tire volume, the number of particles inside increases.
Which statement best explains why tire pressure increases?

choices.A: More gas particles collide with the tire walls.
choices.B: Gas particles stop moving inside the tire.
choices.C: Air changes into a solid metal.
choices.D: The tire produces gravity.

questions.correctAnswerText: More gas particles collide with the tire walls.
questions.explanation: Adding more air increases the number of particles colliding with the tire walls, raising pressure.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A weather station records relative humidity and afternoon thunderstorm reports.
Day | Relative humidity (%) | Thunderstorm reported?
1 | 42 | No
2 | 55 | No
3 | 71 | Yes
4 | 84 | Yes
Which conclusion is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Thunderstorms were reported on the higher-humidity days.
choices.B: Thunderstorms were reported only on the lowest-humidity days.
choices.C: Relative humidity stayed the same every day.
choices.D: Humidity prevented clouds from forming.

questions.correctAnswerText: Thunderstorms were reported on the higher-humidity days.
questions.explanation: Thunderstorms were reported on Days 3 and 4, when humidity was 71% and 84%.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A researcher records the amount of glycogen stored in muscle cells before and after a long-distance run.
Condition | Muscle glycogen (g)
Before run | 410
After run | 170
Which statement is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Muscle glycogen decreased after the long-distance run.
choices.B: Muscle glycogen increased after the long-distance run.
choices.C: Muscle glycogen stayed exactly the same after the run.
choices.D: Muscle cells contained no glycogen before the run.

questions.correctAnswerText: Muscle glycogen decreased after the long-distance run.
questions.explanation: Glycogen decreased from 410 g to 170 g after the run.

questions.prompt: Match each target with the correct tile.

tiles.A: Mass attached to the spring scale
tiles.C: Same spring scale used in every trial

questions.correctAnswerText: T1=Mass attached to the spring scale; T2=Amount the spring stretches; T3=Same spring scale used in every trial
questions.explanation: The mass is changed, the stretch is measured, and the spring scale is controlled.

questions.prompt:
A synapse is a small gap between neurons. When an electrical impulse reaches the end of one neuron, chemicals called neurotransmitters are released. These chemicals cross the synapse and can trigger a response in the next neuron.
Which statement best describes the role of neurotransmitters?

choices.A: They carry signals across synapses between neurons.
choices.B: They form sedimentary rocks.
choices.C: They store water in plant vacuoles.
choices.D: They reflect light from a mirror.

questions.correctAnswerText: They carry signals across synapses between neurons.
questions.explanation: Neurotransmitters cross synapses and pass signals from one neuron to another.

questions.prompt:
A student charges a plastic comb by rubbing it with a cloth. The comb then attracts small neutral paper pieces. The charged comb causes charges inside the paper pieces to shift slightly, making the side near the comb oppositely charged.
Which idea best explains the attraction?

choices.A: Electric charges can attract after charge separation in the paper.
choices.B: The paper pieces become tiny planets.
choices.C: The comb removes all mass from the paper.
choices.D: Electric forces act only in water.

questions.correctAnswerText: Electric charges can attract after charge separation in the paper.
questions.explanation: A charged comb can shift charges in neutral paper, causing attraction.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A weather balloon rises from 500 meters to 2,900 meters in 40 minutes.
What is the average rise rate in meters per minute?
Type your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: The balloon rises 2,400 meters in 40 minutes. 2,400 ÷ 40 = 60 m/min.

questions.prompt:
A researcher studies whether a new physical therapy routine improves knee flexibility after injury. One group follows the new routine, and a similar group follows the standard routine. Both groups exercise for 20 minutes per day. Knee flexibility is measured after six weeks.
Why is the standard-routine group included?

choices.A: It provides a comparison for judging the new routine.
choices.B: It guarantees that no one improves.
choices.C: It changes knee tissue into bone.
choices.D: It removes the need to measure flexibility.

questions.correctAnswerText: It provides a comparison for judging the new routine.
questions.explanation: The standard-routine group allows researchers to compare results with the new-routine group.

questions.prompt:
A student compares how different string tensions affect the pitch of a simple string instrument. The student changes the tension but keeps the string length and string material the same. The frequency of the sound is measured.
What is the dependent variable?

choices.A: Frequency of the sound
choices.B: String tension
choices.C: String length
choices.D: String material

questions.correctAnswerText: Frequency of the sound
questions.explanation: The dependent variable is the measured frequency after changing string tension.

questions.prompt: An object at rest tends to stay at rest unless acted on by a net force. An object in motion tends to continue moving at the same velocity unless acted on by a net force. This tendency is called [D1], and it is related to an object’s [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=inertia; D2=mass
questions.explanation: Inertia is the tendency to resist changes in motion, and more mass means more inertia.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A clinic records average breathing rate before and after a mild asthma treatment.
Condition | Average breathing rate (breaths/min)
Before treatment | 26
After treatment | 18
Which statement is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Average breathing rate decreased after treatment.
choices.B: Average breathing rate increased after treatment.
choices.C: Breathing rate stayed exactly the same after treatment.
choices.D: No patients were breathing before treatment.

questions.correctAnswerText: Average breathing rate decreased after treatment.
questions.explanation: The average breathing rate decreased from 26 to 18 breaths/min.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student records how far a block slides after being pushed across the same surface with different starting speeds.
Starting speed (m/s): 1, 2, 3, 4
Sliding distance (m): 0.4, 1.6, 3.6, 6.4
Which statement is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Higher starting speed is associated with greater sliding distance.
choices.B: Higher starting speed is associated with shorter sliding distance.
choices.C: Sliding distance stays the same for all starting speeds.
choices.D: The block never moves when pushed.

questions.correctAnswerText: Higher starting speed is associated with greater sliding distance.
questions.explanation: Sliding distance increased from 0.4 m to 6.4 m as starting speed increased.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A town compares two land areas after a heat wave.
Land area | Tree cover (%) | Average afternoon air temperature (°C)
Area A | 12 | 38
Area B | 48 | 32
Which TWO statements are best supported by the data?

choices.A: Area B had more tree cover than Area A.
choices.B: Area B had a lower average afternoon air temperature than Area A.
choices.C: Area A had more tree cover than Area B.
choices.D: Area A had the lower average afternoon air temperature.
choices.E: Both areas had exactly the same temperature.

questions.correctAnswerText: Area B had more tree cover than Area A. | Area B had a lower average afternoon air temperature than Area A.
questions.explanation: Area B had 48% tree cover and 32°C, while Area A had 12% tree cover and 38°C.

questions.prompt:
The lymphatic system includes lymph vessels, lymph nodes, and white blood cells. Lymph nodes can trap pathogens and help activate immune cells. The lymphatic system also returns excess fluid from tissues back to the bloodstream.
Which function is described?

choices.A: Immune defense and fluid return
choices.B: Production of sunlight by plants
choices.C: Movement of tectonic plates
choices.D: Reflection of sound waves only

questions.correctAnswerText: Immune defense and fluid return
questions.explanation: Lymph nodes support immune defense, and lymph vessels return excess tissue fluid to the bloodstream.

questions.prompt:
A student compares two forces acting on a book on a table. Gravity pulls the book downward. The table pushes upward with an equal force, so the book does not accelerate.
Which statement best describes the forces on the book?

choices.A: The forces are balanced, so the book remains at rest.
choices.B: The forces are unbalanced, so the book must speed up.
choices.C: Gravity stops acting on the book.
choices.D: The table force turns into light energy.

questions.correctAnswerText: The forces are balanced, so the book remains at rest.
questions.explanation: Equal upward and downward forces result in no acceleration.

questions.prompt:
A model describes how a solar eclipse occurs. The Moon moves between Earth and the Sun. The Moon blocks sunlight from reaching a small part of Earth’s surface.
Which arrangement causes a solar eclipse?

choices.A: Sun, Moon, Earth
choices.B: Moon, Earth, Sun
choices.C: Earth, Sun, Moon
choices.D: Earth, Mars, Moon

questions.correctAnswerText: Sun, Moon, Earth
questions.explanation: A solar eclipse occurs when the Moon is between the Sun and Earth.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A group of 150 people is screened for high blood pressure. Of these people, 39 have high blood pressure.
What percent of the people have high blood pressure?
Type your answer as a percent rounded to the nearest whole number.

A student wants to test how wheel size affects the distance a model vehicle travels after rolling down a ramp. The student changes wheel size but keeps the ramp height, vehicle mass, and starting position the same.
Which factor is the independent variable?

choices.A: Wheel size
choices.B: Distance traveled
choices.C: Ramp height
choices.D: Vehicle mass

questions.correctAnswerText: Wheel size
questions.explanation: The independent variable is the factor intentionally changed by the student.

questions.prompt:
A mutation changes one base in a gene, causing one amino acid in a protein to be different. The changed protein folds incorrectly and cannot perform its normal job in the cell.
Which statement best explains the effect of the mutation?

choices.A: A DNA change can alter protein structure and function.
choices.B: A DNA change always improves protein function.
choices.C: Proteins do not depend on genetic instructions.
choices.D: Amino acids control the orbit of the Moon.

questions.correctAnswerText: A DNA change can alter protein structure and function.
questions.explanation: A change in a gene can change an amino acid sequence, affecting protein folding and function.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student measures the output of a small solar cell at different light intensities.
Light intensity (units): 100, 200, 300, 400
Current produced (mA): 12, 24, 36, 48
Which statement best describes the relationship?

choices.A: Current increased in direct proportion to light intensity.
choices.B: Current decreased as light intensity increased.
choices.C: Current stayed the same at every light intensity.
choices.D: Light intensity and current had an opposite relationship.

questions.correctAnswerText: Current increased in direct proportion to light intensity.
questions.explanation: Current doubled when light intensity doubled, showing a direct proportional relationship.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student compares the average distance of four objects from Earth.
Object | Average distance from Earth
Low-orbit satellite | 400 km
Moon | 384,000 km
Mars at close approach | 56,000,000 km
Sun | 150,000,000 km
Which object listed is closest to Earth?

choices.A: Low-orbit satellite
choices.B: Moon
choices.C: Mars at close approach
choices.D: Sun

questions.correctAnswerText: Low-orbit satellite
questions.explanation: The low-orbit satellite is 400 km from Earth, less than the distances listed for the Moon, Mars, and the Sun.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A researcher records the average reaction time for four age groups during a simple computer test.
Age group | Average reaction time (milliseconds)
Teens | 240
Young adults | 215
Middle-aged adults | 260
Older adults | 330
Which age group had the fastest average reaction time?

choices.A: Teens
choices.B: Young adults
choices.C: Middle-aged adults
choices.D: Older adults

questions.correctAnswerText: Young adults
questions.explanation: The fastest reaction time is the lowest time, 215 milliseconds.

questions.prompt:
A firefighter uses a foam extinguisher on a small fuel fire. The foam covers the fuel surface and reduces contact between the fuel and oxygen in the air.
Which part of the fire triangle is mainly reduced?

choices.A: Oxygen
choices.B: Gravity
choices.C: Sound
choices.D: Magnetism

questions.correctAnswerText: Oxygen
questions.explanation: Foam limits contact between fuel and oxygen, reducing one part of the fire triangle.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A student studies how different saltwater concentrations affect the hatching rate of brine shrimp eggs. The student places equal numbers of eggs into solutions with different salt concentrations and counts hatched shrimp after 48 hours.
Which TWO steps would improve the investigation?

choices.A: Use the same number of eggs in each solution.
choices.B: Keep the hatching time the same for all groups.
choices.C: Change salt concentration and temperature together.
choices.D: Count only the container with the most hatched shrimp.
choices.E: Use a different egg type in each solution.

questions.correctAnswerText: Use the same number of eggs in each solution. | Keep the hatching time the same for all groups.
questions.explanation: Keeping egg number and hatching time constant improves the investigation.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student stretches a spring with different forces.
Force (N): 3, 6, 9, 12
Stretch (cm): 2, 4, 6, 8
How much does the spring stretch when the force is 9 N?

choices.A: 2 cm
choices.B: 4 cm
choices.C: 6 cm
choices.D: 8 cm

questions.correctAnswerText: 6 cm
questions.explanation: The table shows that a 9 N force stretches the spring 6 cm.

questions.prompt:
Barnacles often attach to whales. The barnacles gain access to moving water and food particles. The whale is usually not helped or harmed significantly by the barnacles.
Which relationship is described?

choices.A: Commensalism
choices.B: Predation
choices.C: Parasitism
choices.D: Competition

questions.correctAnswerText: Commensalism
questions.explanation: Commensalism occurs when one organism benefits and the other is not significantly helped or harmed.

questions.prompt:
The pancreas releases digestive enzymes into the small intestine. These enzymes help break down carbohydrates, proteins, and fats into smaller molecules that can be absorbed.
Which statement best describes this function of the pancreas?

choices.A: It produces enzymes that help digest food molecules.
choices.B: It exchanges gases between air and blood.
choices.C: It forms the hard outer layer of teeth.
choices.D: It stores most genetic information in neurons.

questions.correctAnswerText: It produces enzymes that help digest food molecules.
questions.explanation: The pancreas releases enzymes into the small intestine to help digest carbohydrates, proteins, and fats.
